skill.name: session-handoff
skill.description: Create, validate, list, and resume structured handoff documents for long AI coding sessions.
skill.summary: Preserves project state so another AI agent can continue work with clear context.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, filesystem, environment, and network patterns. Review confirmed local Python helper scripts with hardcoded git subprocess calls and filesystem access, while network and environment matches are evaluation fixtures or sample text. No malicious exfiltration or prompt injection text was found, but handoff documents can contain sensitive project context and should be treated as trusted workspace artifacts.

medium_findings.title: Local Git Subprocess Execution
medium_findings.description: The runtime scripts execute git commands through subprocess.run. The command arrays are hardcoded and do not use a shell, so command injection evidence was not found, but publication should disclose local command execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct subprocess.run calls exist in runtime scripts. Confidence is high because the calls are real, but risk is moderated by shell-free hardcoded argument lists.

medium_findings.title: Persistent Handoff Files May Contain Sensitive Context
medium_findings.description: The skill creates Markdown handoff files under .claude/handoffs and pre-fills project path, branch, commit messages, and modified file names. The validator checks for common secrets, but user-entered handoff content may still persist sensitive business or implementation context.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file creation and metadata capture are explicit. The risk depends on user content and repository handling, so it is not evidence of malicious collection.

medium_findings.title: Untrusted Handoff Content Can Influence Agent Behavior
medium_findings.description: The resume workflow instructs the agent to read a handoff document completely and continue from its next steps. No prompt-injection text was found in the bundled files, but handoffs imported from a repository should be treated as untrusted input.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow consumes Markdown handoffs as operational context. No malicious bundled payload was found, so the issue is a contextual prompt-injection exposure.

low_findings.title: Evaluation Fixtures Trigger Static Rules
low_findings.description: Several static findings come from evaluation setup files and sample handoff content, including test-only subprocess calls, temporary directories, environment variable names, and a public documentation URL. These are not part of the normal handoff runtime path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are in an evaluation harness and generated sample project text. They show test data and fixture setup, not production exfiltration or credential access.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Examples Are Not Executed
low_findings.description: Many external command matches are Markdown examples showing how to run local helper scripts or inspect git state. They should be disclosed as expected usage, but they are not hidden execution paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is inside Markdown code blocks and usage instructions. The commands are visible to the user and align with the skill purpose.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run for Git Introspection
dangerous_patterns.description: Local helper scripts call git through subprocess.run to inspect repository state. The calls are shell-free, but they still execute local commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess pattern is directly present. Hardcoded list arguments reduce command injection likelihood.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Markdown Read and Write Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts read existing handoffs and write new handoff documents. This is expected behavior, but it can preserve sensitive context on disk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The read and write operations are explicit and central to the skill. The risk is data handling, not hidden modification.

content.value_statement: Long AI coding sessions lose context when a session ends. This skill creates structured handoff documents so Claude, Codex, or Claude Code can resume with clear next steps.
actual_capabilities: Generates timestamped handoff Markdown files under .claude/handoffs.
Pre-fills project path, git branch, recent commits, and changed files.
Validates handoffs for required sections, TODOs, file references, and common secrets.
Lists existing handoffs with titles, dates, completion status, and file paths.
Checks handoff staleness against current git history and referenced files.
Supports chained handoffs that link related work sessions.
limitations: Requires local Python scripts and workspace filesystem access.
Git-based metadata works only inside a git repository.
Secret detection uses patterns and cannot guarantee full coverage.
Handoff quality depends on the user or agent completing the template accurately.

use_cases.title: Pause a Long Coding Task
use_cases.description: Capture current state, modified files, decisions, blockers, and immediate next steps before ending a session.

use_cases.title: Resume Work From a Previous Agent
use_cases.description: Find the latest handoff, check if it is stale, and continue from the documented next action.

use_cases.title: Maintain Context Across Project Milestones
use_cases.description: Create chained handoffs after major milestones so future sessions can trace decisions and progress.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a handoff for the current work session. Include the current status, modified files, decisions, blockers, and next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a Handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate the handoff file and summarize any TODOs, missing sections, stale references, or possible secrets.

prompt_templates.title: Resume From the Latest Handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the latest handoff, check staleness, read it fully, and tell me the first safe action to take.

prompt_templates.title: Chain a Milestone Handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a continuation handoff linked to the previous one. Emphasize new decisions, completed tasks, changed assumptions, and next risks.

output_examples.input: Create a handoff for this authentication refactor.
output_examples.output: Creates a timestamped handoff file with session metadata, current state, completed work, pending tasks, decisions, and environment notes.

output_examples.input: Resume from the most recent handoff.
output_examples.output: Lists available handoffs, checks staleness, reads the selected handoff, and identifies the first verified next step.

output_examples.input: Validate this handoff before I stop.
output_examples.output: Reports quality score, remaining placeholders, missing required sections, possible secrets, and broken file references.

best_practices: Run validation before sharing or committing a handoff.
Record environment variable names only, never secret values.
Review imported handoffs for untrusted instructions before following them.
anti_patterns: Do not store API keys, tokens, passwords, or private keys in handoffs.
Do not resume from a stale handoff without checking current git state.
Do not leave vague TODOs where the next agent needs concrete actions.

faq.question: Where are handoff files stored?
faq.answer: The skill stores generated handoffs in .claude/handoffs within the current project.

faq.question: Does this skill send data to a remote service?
faq.answer: No runtime network call was found in the reviewed scripts. The skill operates on local files and git metadata.

faq.question: Can it detect secrets in handoffs?
faq.answer: It checks common secret patterns such as API keys, passwords, tokens, and private key markers.

faq.question: Can it resume work automatically?
faq.answer: It provides the context and checklist for resumption. The agent should still verify current project state first.

faq.question: Does it require git?
faq.answer: Git is not required for all features, but branch, commit, and staleness checks depend on git.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish handoffs?
faq.answer: Only publish reviewed handoffs. They may contain project paths, commit messages, file names, and sensitive context.
